title: Coffee and heart health: How many cups of caffeinated coffee are safe to drink each day?
excerpt: Statement Highlights: The latest research indicates that consuming up to 400 mg of caffeine/day (equivalent to up to 5, 8-ounce cups of caffeinated coffee per day) is generally safe for most adults and appears to be linked to a lower risk of several ...
content_html:
Statement Highlights:

  • The latest research indicates that consuming up to 400 mg of caffeine/day (equivalent to up to 5, 8-ounce cups of caffeinated coffee per day) is generally safe for most adults and appears to be linked to a lower risk of several cardiovascular conditions, including heart failure, heart disease and stroke in some individuals.
  • However, higher amounts of caffeine, such as levels found in energy drinks including energy shots, may increase the risk of high blood pressure and/or irregular heart rhythm or arrhythmia.
  • More research is needed to better understand caffeine’s impact on the body, how different sources of caffeine impact heart health and how its impact may vary among different people, according to the scientific statement.

Embargoed until 4:00 a.m. CT / 5:00 a.m. ET Monday, July 20, 2026

DALLAS, July 20, 2026 — Previous studies have found that caffeine is the world’s most popular stimulant and psychoactive sub­stance, and coffee is the most common way people consume caffeine. The latest research supports the conclusion that for most adults, consuming up to 400 mg of caffeine/day (or up to 5, 8-ounce cups of caffeinated coffee per day) is safe and appears to be linked to a lower risk of cardiovascular disease for some individuals. However, higher doses of caffeine, such as levels found in energy drinks including energy shots, may cause cardiovascular harm, according to a new American Heart Association scientific statement, “Caffeine and Cardiovascular Disease,” published today in the American Heart Association’s flagship peer-reviewed scientific journal Circulation.

“Caffeine consumed in coffee is a key part of daily life for millions of people, and in our review of the most recent research, for most adults, intake of up to 400 mg of caffeine/day, the equivalent of up to 5 cups of caffeinated coffee per day without added sugars or fillers, is safe and does not increase cardiovascular risk,” said Chair of the scientific statement volunteer writing group Gregory M. Marcus, M.D., M.A.S., FAHA, a professor of medicine at the University of California, San Francisco School of Medicine and the associate chief of Cardiology for Research at the University of California, San Francisco Health. “However, high doses of caffeine, such as those found in energy drinks including energy shots, may have harmful effects on the heart and should be avoided.”

The statement authors note that studying the effects of caffeine on the cardiovascular system is challenging. Coffee is the main source of caffeine in most research studies, and the observed cardiovascular effects may be due to other compounds in coffee. For example, experimental studies suggest that the bioactive compounds in coffee may have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help explain some health benefits. It’s also difficult to separate the effects of caffeine from other ingredients usually added to coffee, such as milk, cream, flavored syrups and/or sugar. In addition, most studies on caffeine are observational, meaning they cannot prove cause and effect. 

Although coffee is the main source of caffeine for most adults, tea, chocolate, soda and energy drinks, as well as over-the-counter and prescription medications, also contribute to overall caffeine intake. More research is needed about other products with caffeine in order to understand caffeine content and caffeine’s impact on cardiovascular health.

The new scientific statement details the most recent research primarily on caffeinated coffee and the potential impact of caffeine in coffee on high blood pressure, cholesterol, Type 2 diabetes, coronary heart disease, stroke, heart failure, atrial fibrillation, other arrhythmias and other cardiovascular conditions.

Caffeine and cardiovascular health: key things to know

  • Based on the results of the latest research studies reviewed, consuming up to 400 mg of caffeine per day, or no more than 3 to 5, 8-ounce cups of regular black caffeinated coffee per day (without sugars, sweeteners, flavors or added fillers), is considered safe for most adults. A regular brewed, non-specialty, caffeinated coffee typically contains 9.4-20.6 mg of caffeine per fluid ounce.
  • Consuming caffeinated coffee without added sugars, flavors or cream was linked to a lower risk of Type 2 diabetes, heart disease, stroke, heart failure and some irregular heart rhythms.
  • Randomized trials, the most rigorous and reliable type of study design, have shown that caffeine consumption in coffee is associated with a lower risk of atrial fibrillation but also a higher risk of premature ventricular contractions.
  • Adding sugar, flavored syrups, milk and/or cream to coffee likely reduces its potential health benefits, and more research is needed to understand the impact of these additives.
  • Higher caffeine intake, such as levels found in energy drinks including energy shots, was associated with cardiovascular harm, such as increased risk of high blood pressure and irregular heart rhythm. Energy drink shots may contain 40-69 mg of caffeine per fluid ounce, 3-4 times more caffeine than regular caffeinated coffee.

How does caffeine affect the body?

Caffeine consumed in coffee is metabolized by the liver and generally reaches peak concentration for most people within an hour. However, how quickly individuals process caffeine depends on their genetics, metabolism, age and past caffeine use. When more caffeinated coffee is consumed regularly, some people may develop a tolerance to higher levels of caffeine. Other people may feel stronger effects from the same amount of caffeine. Short-term effects of caffeine may include temporary increases in blood pressure, heart rate, blood sugar and alertness. Some people may experience heart palpitations and/or sleep disruption.

Specific health impacts of caffeine:

  • Effects on blood pressure: Studies have found that caffeinated coffee consumption may affect blood pressure differently depending on how much is consumed. In people with optimal blood pressure, drinking 1–3 cups a day was associated with an increased risk of developing high blood pressure, while drinking more than 3 cups per day was associated with a lower risk. High doses of caffeine (such as those from energy drinks or energy shots) can significantly increase blood pressure, especially in people who already have high blood pressure.
  • Effects on Type 2 diabetes: Research suggests that caffeinated coffee may reduce insulin sensitivity in the short term. In addition, drinking black, caffeinated coffee (no additives, flavors or sweeteners) regularly was linked to a lower risk of developing Type 2 diabetes. However, this benefit may be due to compounds in coffee other than caffeine. More research is needed to clarify the impact on Type 2 diabetes of caffeinated coffee vs. various compounds in coffee.
  • Effects on cholesterol: Data from randomized clinical trials indicate cafestol, a component in both caffeinated and decaffeinated coffee, was associated with higher levels of low-density lipoprotein (also known as “bad” cholesterol). Cafestol is present in unfiltered coffee (such as espresso, French press, Turkish coffee or boiled coffee) but not present in coffee brewed with paper filters or instant coffee. More research is needed to understand which products have higher levels of cafestol and their mechanisms.  
  • Effects on heart rhythm: The most recent analyses of health measures with self-reported information from participants have found that drinking 1-3 cups of caffeinated coffee per day was not associated with an increased risk of atrial fibrillation or abnormal heart rhythm. However, caffeinated coffee at that same level may be associated with more early beats from the lower chamber of the heart, called premature ventricular contractions (or PVCs). Very high doses of caffeine (such as those in caffeinated energy drinks or energy shots) have been linked to abnormal heart rhythm in people usually considered to have low cardiovascular disease risk, such as healthy adults younger than age 30.
  • Effects on heart disease and stroke: Drinking 2-4 cups of caffeinated coffee per day was linked to a lower risk of heart disease, heart failure and stroke. However, drinking more than 4 cups of caffeinated coffee a day may increase the risk of heart failure.

Not all sources of caffeine are the same

More research is necessary to investigate the different health impacts of various types of caffeinated coffee and other products with caffeine, such as tea, soda, energy drinks and energy shots, and foods. There have been fewer studies focused on tea vs. coffee; however, tea has been associated with reduced risk of atrial fibrillation, heart failure and stroke, similar to caffeinated coffee. Energy drinks, bars, gels and caffeine-based supplements often contain other added ingredients that may increase the absorption of caffeine, may accelerate its effects on the body, may raise blood pressure and increase the risk of cardiovascular harm.

More randomized controlled trials investigating the effects of caffeine in coffee and other products that have caffeine on cardiovascular health are needed to better understand how caffeine affects different people and how different sources of caffeine impact heart health. The writing group emphasized that there is also a need for research focused on caffeinated energy drinks and other popular products containing caffeine. Due to the lack of sufficient, rigorous data to develop guidance on recommendations for caffeine intake, caffeine was not referenced in the Association’s 2026 Dietary Guidance to Improve Cardiovascular Health. 

“Although research suggests that caffeine consumption may be associated with certain cardiovascular benefits for some people, it’s important to remember that there is no ‘one-size-fits-all’ strategy for safe caffeine consumption. People can respond very differently to caffeine based on various factors, such as age, medications, underlying health conditions, genetics and how quickly their bodies metabolize it. What may be a reasonable amount for one person could cause unwanted effects, such as heart palpitations, anxiety or sleep disruption, in another. That’s why it’s important to pay attention to how your body responds to caffeine and talk with your healthcare team about what is right for you,” Marcus said.

This scientific statement was prepared by the volunteer writing group on behalf of the American Heart Association’s Council on Lifestyle and Cardiometabolic Health; the Council on Clinical Cardiology; and the Stroke Council. American Heart Association scientific statements promote greater awareness about cardiovascular diseases and stroke issues and help facilitate informed health care decisions. Scientific statements outline what is currently known about a topic and what areas need additional research. While scientific statements inform the development of guidelines, they do not make treatment recommendations. American Heart Association guidelines provide the Association’s official clinical practice recommendations.

Co-authors are Vice Chair Frank B. Hu, M.D., M.P.H., Ph.D., FAHA; Rob M. van Dam, Ph.D.; Marilyn C. Cornelis, Ph.D.; Thomas A. Dewland, M.D.; JungHee Kang, Ph.D., M.P.H., R.N.; Susanna C. In addition, most studies on caffeine are observational, meaning they cannot prove cause and effect.

sentences.en: The statement authors note that studying the effects of caffeine on the cardiovascular system is challenging.
sentences.zh: 该声明的作者指出，研究咖啡因对心血管系统的影响具有挑战性。

sentences.en: Coffee is the main source of caffeine in most research studies, and the observed cardiovascular effects may be due to other compounds in coffee.
sentences.zh: 在大多数研究中，咖啡是咖啡因的主要来源，观察到的心血管影响可能是由于咖啡中的其他化合物造成的。

sentences.en: For example, experimental studies suggest that the bioactive compounds in coffee may have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help explain some health benefits.
sentences.zh: 例如，实验研究表明咖啡中的生物活性化合物可能具有抗氧化和抗炎特性，这可能有助于解释一些健康益处。

sentences.en: It’s also difficult to separate the effects of caffeine from other ingredients usually added to coffee, such as milk, cream, flavored syrups and/or sugar.
sentences.zh: 也很难将咖啡因的影响与通常添加到咖啡中的其他成分（例如牛奶、奶油、调味糖浆和/或糖）分开。

sentences.en: In addition, most studies on caffeine are observational, meaning they cannot prove cause and effect.
sentences.zh: 此外，大多数关于咖啡因的研究都是观察性的，这意味着它们无法证明因果关系。

blocks.html_en: Although coffee is the main source of caffeine for most adults, tea, chocolate, soda and energy drinks, as well as over-the-counter and prescription medications, also contribute to overall caffeine intake. More research is needed about other products with caffeine in order to understand caffeine content and caffeine’s impact on cardiovascular health.

sentences.en: Although coffee is the main source of caffeine for most adults, tea, chocolate, soda and energy drinks, as well as over-the-counter and prescription medications, also contribute to overall caffeine intake.
sentences.zh: 虽然咖啡是大多数成年人咖啡因的主要来源，但茶、巧克力、苏打水和能量饮料以及非处方药和处方药也有助于咖啡因的总体摄入量。

sentences.en: More research is needed about other products with caffeine in order to understand caffeine content and caffeine’s impact on cardiovascular health.
sentences.zh: 需要对其他含有咖啡因的产品进行更多研究，以了解咖啡因含量和咖啡因对心血管健康的影响。

blocks.html_en: The new scientific statement details the most recent research primarily on caffeinated coffee and the potential impact of caffeine in coffee on high blood pressure, cholesterol, Type 2 diabetes, coronary heart disease, stroke, heart failure, atrial fibrillation, other arrhythmias and other cardiovascular conditions.

sentences.en: The new scientific statement details the most recent research primarily on caffeinated coffee and the potential impact of caffeine in coffee on high blood pressure, cholesterol, Type 2 diabetes, coronary heart disease, stroke, heart failure, atrial fibrillation, other arrhythmias and other cardiovascular conditions.
sentences.zh: 新的科学声明详细介绍了主要针对含咖啡因咖啡的最新研究，以及咖啡中咖啡因对高血压、胆固醇、2型糖尿病、冠心病、中风、心力衰竭、心房颤动、其他心律失常和其他心血管疾病的潜在影响。

blocks.html_en: Caffeine and cardiovascular health: key things to know

sentences.en: Caffeine and cardiovascular health: key things to know
sentences.zh: 咖啡因和心血管健康：需要了解的关键事项

blocks.html_en: • Based on the results of the latest research studies reviewed, consuming up to 400 mg of caffeine per day, or no more than 3 to 5, 8-ounce cups of regular black caffeinated coffee per day (without sugars, sweeteners, flavors or added fillers), is considered safe for most adults. A regular brewed, non-specialty, caffeinated coffee typically contains 9.4-20.6 mg of caffeine per fluid ounce.

sentences.en: Based on the results of the latest research studies reviewed, consuming up to 400 mg of caffeine per day, or no more than 3 to 5, 8-ounce cups of regular black caffeinated coffee per day (without sugars, sweeteners, flavors or added fillers), is considered safe for most adults.
sentences.zh: 根据最新审查的研究结果，对于大多数成年人来说，每天摄入最多 400 毫克咖啡因，或每天不超过 3 到 5 杯 8 盎司的普通黑咖啡因咖啡（不含糖、甜味剂、香料或添加填充剂）被认为是安全的。

sentences.en: A regular brewed, non-specialty, caffeinated coffee typically contains 9.4-20.6 mg of caffeine per fluid ounce.
sentences.zh: 常规冲泡的非特制含咖啡因咖啡通常每盎司含有 9.4-20.6 毫克咖啡因。

blocks.html_en: • Consuming caffeinated coffee without added sugars, flavors or cream was linked to a lower risk of Type 2 diabetes, heart disease, stroke, heart failure and some irregular heart rhythms.

sentences.en: Consuming caffeinated coffee without added sugars, flavors or cream was linked to a lower risk of Type 2 diabetes, heart disease, stroke, heart failure and some irregular heart rhythms.
sentences.zh: 饮用不添加糖、香料或奶油的含咖啡因咖啡可以降低患 2 型糖尿病、心脏病、中风、心力衰竭和某些心律不齐的风险。

blocks.html_en: • Randomized trials, the most rigorous and reliable type of study design, have shown that caffeine consumption in coffee is associated with a lower risk of atrial fibrillation but also a higher risk of premature ventricular contractions.

sentences.en: Randomized trials, the most rigorous and reliable type of study design, have shown that caffeine consumption in coffee is associated with a lower risk of atrial fibrillation but also a higher risk of premature ventricular contractions.
sentences.zh: 随机试验是最严格、最可靠的研究设计类型，表明咖啡中的咖啡因摄入与心房颤动的较低风险相关，但也与室性早搏的较高风险相关。

blocks.html_en: • Adding sugar, flavored syrups, milk and/or cream to coffee likely reduces its potential health benefits, and more research is needed to understand the impact of these additives.

sentences.en: Adding sugar, flavored syrups, milk and/or cream to coffee likely reduces its potential health benefits, and more research is needed to understand the impact of these additives.
sentences.zh: 在咖啡中添加糖、调味糖浆、牛奶和/或奶油可能会降低其潜在的健康益处，需要更多的研究来了解这些添加剂的影响。

blocks.html_en: • Higher caffeine intake, such as levels found in energy drinks including energy shots, was associated with cardiovascular harm, such as increased risk of high blood pressure and irregular heart rhythm. Energy drink shots may contain 40-69 mg of caffeine per fluid ounce, 3-4 times more caffeine than regular caffeinated coffee.

sentences.en: Higher caffeine intake, such as levels found in energy drinks including energy shots, was associated with cardiovascular harm, such as increased risk of high blood pressure and irregular heart rhythm.
sentences.zh: 较高的咖啡因摄入量（例如能量饮料中的咖啡因含量）与心血管危害相关，例如高血压和心律不齐的风险增加。

sentences.en: Energy drink shots may contain 40-69 mg of caffeine per fluid ounce, 3-4 times more caffeine than regular caffeinated coffee.
sentences.zh: 每盎司能量饮料可能含有 40-69 毫克咖啡因，是普通含咖啡因咖啡的 3-4 倍。

blocks.html_en: How does caffeine affect the body?

sentences.en: How does caffeine affect the body?
sentences.zh: 咖啡因如何影响身体？

blocks.html_en: Caffeine consumed in coffee is metabolized by the liver and generally reaches peak concentration for most people within an hour. However, how quickly individuals process caffeine depends on their genetics, metabolism, age and past caffeine use. When more caffeinated coffee is consumed regularly, some people may develop a tolerance to higher levels of caffeine. Other people may feel stronger effects from the same amount of caffeine. Short-term effects of caffeine may include temporary increases in blood pressure, heart rate, blood sugar and alertness. Some people may experience heart palpitations and/or sleep disruption.

sentences.en: Caffeine consumed in coffee is metabolized by the liver and generally reaches peak concentration for most people within an hour.
sentences.zh: 咖啡中的咖啡因由肝脏代谢，对于大多数人来说通常在一小时内达到峰值浓度。

sentences.en: However, how quickly individuals process caffeine depends on their genetics, metabolism, age and past caffeine use.
sentences.zh: 然而，个人处理咖啡因的速度取决于他们的遗传、新陈代谢、年龄和过去的咖啡因使用情况。

sentences.en: When more caffeinated coffee is consumed regularly, some people may develop a tolerance to higher levels of caffeine.
sentences.zh: 当经常饮用更多含咖啡因的咖啡时，有些人可能会对更高含量的咖啡因产生耐受性。

sentences.en: Other people may feel stronger effects from the same amount of caffeine.
sentences.zh: 其他人可能会从相同量的咖啡因中感受到更强的效果。

sentences.en: Short-term effects of caffeine may include temporary increases in blood pressure, heart rate, blood sugar and alertness.
sentences.zh: 咖啡因的短期影响可能包括暂时升高血压、心率、血糖和警觉性。

sentences.en: Some people may experience heart palpitations and/or sleep disruption.
sentences.zh: 有些人可能会出现心悸和/或睡眠中断。

blocks.html_en: Specific health impacts of caffeine:

sentences.en: Specific health impacts of caffeine:
sentences.zh: 咖啡因对健康的具体影响：

blocks.html_en: • Effects on blood pressure: Studies have found that caffeinated coffee consumption may affect blood pressure differently depending on how much is consumed. In people with optimal blood pressure, drinking 1–3 cups a day was associated with an increased risk of developing high blood pressure, while drinking more than 3 cups per day was associated with a lower risk. High doses of caffeine (such as those from energy drinks or energy shots) can significantly increase blood pressure, especially in people who already have high blood pressure.

sentences.en: Effects on blood pressure: Studies have found that caffeinated coffee consumption may affect blood pressure differently depending on how much is consumed.
sentences.zh: 对血压的影响：研究发现，饮用含咖啡因的咖啡可能会对血压产生不同的影响，具体取决于饮用量。

sentences.en: In people with optimal blood pressure, drinking 1–3 cups a day was associated with an increased risk of developing high blood pressure, while drinking more than 3 cups per day was associated with a lower risk.
sentences.zh: 对于血压处于最佳水平的人来说，每天喝 1-3 杯会增加患高血压的风险，而每天喝 3 杯以上则会降低患高血压的风险。

sentences.en: High doses of caffeine (such as those from energy drinks or energy shots) can significantly increase blood pressure, especially in people who already have high blood pressure.
sentences.zh: 高剂量的咖啡因（例如能量饮料或能量注射中的咖啡因）会显着升高血压，尤其是对于已经患有高血压的人。

blocks.html_en: • Effects on Type 2 diabetes: Research suggests that caffeinated coffee may reduce insulin sensitivity in the short term. In addition, drinking black, caffeinated coffee (no additives, flavors or sweeteners) regularly was linked to a lower risk of developing Type 2 diabetes. However, this benefit may be due to compounds in coffee other than caffeine. More research is needed to clarify the impact on Type 2 diabetes of caffeinated coffee vs. various compounds in coffee.

sentences.en: Effects on Type 2 diabetes: Research suggests that caffeinated coffee may reduce insulin sensitivity in the short term.
sentences.zh: 对 2 型糖尿病的影响：研究表明，含咖啡因的咖啡可能会在短期内降低胰岛素敏感性。

sentences.en: In addition, drinking black, caffeinated coffee (no additives, flavors or sweeteners) regularly was linked to a lower risk of developing Type 2 diabetes.
sentences.zh: 此外，经常饮用含咖啡因的黑咖啡（不含添加剂、香料或甜味剂）与降低患 2 型糖尿病的风险有关。

sentences.en: However, this benefit may be due to compounds in coffee other than caffeine.
sentences.zh: 然而，这种好处可能是由于咖啡中除咖啡因以外的化合物。

sentences.en: More research is needed to clarify the impact on Type 2 diabetes of caffeinated coffee vs. various compounds in coffee.
sentences.zh: 需要更多的研究来阐明含咖啡因的咖啡与咖啡中的各种化合物对 2 型糖尿病的影响。

blocks.html_en: • Effects on cholesterol: Data from randomized clinical trials indicate cafestol, a component in both caffeinated and decaffeinated coffee, was associated with higher levels of low-density lipoprotein (also known as “bad” cholesterol). Cafestol is present in unfiltered coffee (such as espresso, French press, Turkish coffee or boiled coffee) but not present in coffee brewed with paper filters or instant coffee. More research is needed to understand which products have higher levels of cafestol and their mechanisms.

sentences.en: Effects on cholesterol: Data from randomized clinical trials indicate cafestol, a component in both caffeinated and decaffeinated coffee, was associated with higher levels of low-density lipoprotein (also known as “bad” cholesterol).
sentences.zh: 对胆固醇的影响：随机临床试验的数据表明，咖啡醇（含咖啡因和不含咖啡因的咖啡中的一种成分）与较高水平的低密度脂蛋白（也称为“坏”胆固醇）有关。

sentences.en: Cafestol is present in unfiltered coffee (such as espresso, French press, Turkish coffee or boiled coffee) but not present in coffee brewed with paper filters or instant coffee.
sentences.zh: 咖啡醇存在于未过滤的咖啡（例如浓缩咖啡、法式滤压咖啡、土耳其咖啡或煮咖啡）中，但不存在于用滤纸冲泡的咖啡或速溶咖啡中。

sentences.en: More research is needed to understand which products have higher levels of cafestol and their mechanisms.
sentences.zh: 需要更多的研究来了解哪些产品的咖啡醇含量较高及其机制。

blocks.html_en: • Effects on heart rhythm: The most recent analyses of health measures with self-reported information from participants have found that drinking 1-3 cups of caffeinated coffee per day was not associated with an increased risk of atrial fibrillation or abnormal heart rhythm. However, caffeinated coffee at that same level may be associated with more early beats from the lower chamber of the heart, called premature ventricular contractions (or PVCs). Very high doses of caffeine (such as those in caffeinated energy drinks or energy shots) have been linked to abnormal heart rhythm in people usually considered to have low cardiovascular disease risk, such as healthy adults younger than age 30.

sentences.en: Effects on heart rhythm: The most recent analyses of health measures with self-reported information from participants have found that drinking 1-3 cups of caffeinated coffee per day was not associated with an increased risk of atrial fibrillation or abnormal heart rhythm.
sentences.zh: 对心律的影响：最新的健康测量分析以及参与者自我报告的信息发现，每天喝 1-3 杯含咖啡因的咖啡与心房颤动或心律异常的风险增加无关。

sentences.en: However, caffeinated coffee at that same level may be associated with more early beats from the lower chamber of the heart, called premature ventricular contractions (or PVCs).
sentences.zh: 然而，相同水平的含咖啡因咖啡可能与心脏下腔更多的早期搏动有关，称为室性早搏（或 PVC）。

sentences.en: Very high doses of caffeine (such as those in caffeinated energy drinks or energy shots) have been linked to abnormal heart rhythm in people usually considered to have low cardiovascular disease risk, such as healthy adults younger than age 30.
sentences.zh: 极高剂量的咖啡因（例如含咖啡因的能量饮料或能量注射液中的咖啡因）与通常被认为心血管疾病风险较低的人群（例如 30 岁以下的健康成年人）心律失常有关。

blocks.html_en: • Effects on heart disease and stroke: Drinking 2-4 cups of caffeinated coffee per day was linked to a lower risk of heart disease, heart failure and stroke. However, drinking more than 4 cups of caffeinated coffee a day may increase the risk of heart failure.

sentences.en: Effects on heart disease and stroke: Drinking 2-4 cups of caffeinated coffee per day was linked to a lower risk of heart disease, heart failure and stroke.
sentences.zh: 对心脏病和中风的影响：每天喝 2-4 杯含咖啡因的咖啡可以降低患心脏病、心力衰竭和中风的风险。

sentences.en: However, drinking more than 4 cups of caffeinated coffee a day may increase the risk of heart failure.
sentences.zh: 然而，每天喝超过 4 杯含咖啡因的咖啡可能会增加心力衰竭的风险。

blocks.html_en: Not all sources of caffeine are the same

sentences.en: Not all sources of caffeine are the same
sentences.zh: 并非所有咖啡因的来源都是相同的

blocks.html_en: More research is necessary to investigate the different health impacts of various types of caffeinated coffee and other products with caffeine, such as tea, soda, energy drinks and energy shots, and foods. There have been fewer studies focused on tea vs. coffee; however, tea has been associated with reduced risk of atrial fibrillation, heart failure and stroke, similar to caffeinated coffee. Energy drinks, bars, gels and caffeine-based supplements often contain other added ingredients that may increase the absorption of caffeine, may accelerate its effects on the body, may raise blood pressure and increase the risk of cardiovascular harm.

sentences.en: More research is necessary to investigate the different health impacts of various types of caffeinated coffee and other products with caffeine, such as tea, soda, energy drinks and energy shots, and foods.
sentences.zh: 需要更多的研究来调查各种类型的含咖啡因咖啡和其他含有咖啡因的产品，如茶、苏打水、能量饮料和能量饮料以及食物对健康的不同影响。

sentences.en: There have been fewer studies focused on tea vs. coffee; however, tea has been associated with reduced risk of atrial fibrillation, heart failure and stroke, similar to caffeinated coffee.
sentences.zh: 针对茶与咖啡的研究较少。然而，与含咖啡因的咖啡类似，茶可以降低心房颤动、心力衰竭和中风的风险。

sentences.en: Energy drinks, bars, gels and caffeine-based supplements often contain other added ingredients that may increase the absorption of caffeine, may accelerate its effects on the body, may raise blood pressure and increase the risk of cardiovascular harm.
sentences.zh: 能量饮料、能量棒、凝胶和咖啡因补充剂通常含有其他添加成分，这些成分可能会增加咖啡因的吸收，可能会加速其对身体的影响，可能会升高血压并增加心血管伤害的风险。

blocks.html_en: More randomized controlled trials investigating the effects of caffeine in coffee and other products that have caffeine on cardiovascular health are needed to better understand how caffeine affects different people and how different sources of caffeine impact heart health. The writing group emphasized that there is also a need for research focused on caffeinated energy drinks and other popular products containing caffeine. Due to the lack of sufficient, rigorous data to develop guidance on recommendations for caffeine intake, caffeine was not referenced in the Association’s 2026 Dietary Guidance to Improve Cardiovascular Health.

sentences.en: More randomized controlled trials investigating the effects of caffeine in coffee and other products that have caffeine on cardiovascular health are needed to better understand how caffeine affects different people and how different sources of caffeine impact heart health.
sentences.zh: 需要更多随机对照试验来调查咖啡和其他含有咖啡因的产品中的咖啡因对心血管健康的影响，以更好地了解咖啡因如何影响不同的人以及不同来源的咖啡因如何影响心脏健康。

sentences.en: The writing group emphasized that there is also a need for research focused on caffeinated energy drinks and other popular products containing caffeine.
sentences.zh: 写作小组强调，还需要针对含咖啡因的能量饮料和其他含有咖啡因的流行产品进行研究。

sentences.en: Due to the lack of sufficient, rigorous data to develop guidance on recommendations for caffeine intake, caffeine was not referenced in the Association’s 2026 Dietary Guidance to Improve Cardiovascular Health.
sentences.zh: 由于缺乏足够、严格的数据来制定咖啡因摄入建议指南，该协会的 2026 年改善心血管健康饮食指南中没有提及咖啡因。

blocks.html_en: “Although research suggests that caffeine consumption may be associated with certain cardiovascular benefits for some people, it’s important to remember that there is no ‘one-size-fits-all’ strategy for safe caffeine consumption. People can respond very differently to caffeine based on various factors, such as age, medications, underlying health conditions, genetics and how quickly their bodies metabolize it. What may be a reasonable amount for one person could cause unwanted effects, such as heart palpitations, anxiety or sleep disruption, in another. That’s why it’s important to pay attention to how your body responds to caffeine and talk with your healthcare team about what is right for you,” Marcus said.

sentences.en: “Although research suggests that caffeine consumption may be associated with certain cardiovascular benefits for some people, it’s important to remember that there is no ‘one-size-fits-all’ strategy for safe caffeine consumption.
sentences.zh: “尽管研究表明，对某些人来说，摄入咖啡因可能与某些心血管益处有关，但重要的是要记住，安全摄入咖啡因并没有‘一刀切’的策略。

sentences.en: People can respond very differently to caffeine based on various factors, such as age, medications, underlying health conditions, genetics and how quickly their bodies metabolize it.
sentences.zh: 人们对咖啡因的反应可能因多种因素而异，例如年龄、药物、潜在健康状况、遗传以及身体代谢咖啡因的速度。

sentences.en: What may be a reasonable amount for one person could cause unwanted effects, such as heart palpitations, anxiety or sleep disruption, in another.
sentences.zh: 对一个人来说合理的剂量可能会对另一个人造成不良影响，例如心悸、焦虑或睡眠中断。

sentences.en: That’s why it’s important to pay attention to how your body responds to caffeine and talk with your healthcare team about what is right for you,” Marcus said.
sentences.zh: 这就是为什么重要的是要注意你的身体对咖啡因的反应，并与你的医疗团队讨论什么适合你，”马库斯说。

blocks.html_en: This scientific statement was prepared by the volunteer writing group on behalf of the American Heart Association’s Council on Lifestyle and Cardiometabolic Health; the Council on Clinical Cardiology; and the Stroke Council. American Heart Association scientific statements promote greater awareness about cardiovascular diseases and stroke issues and help facilitate informed health care decisions. Scientific statements outline what is currently known about a topic and what areas need additional research. While scientific statements inform the development of guidelines, they do not make treatment recommendations. American Heart Association guidelines provide the Association’s official clinical practice recommendations.

blocks.html_en: The American Heart Association is a relentless force for a world of longer, healthier lives. Dedicated to ensuring equitable health in all communities, the organization has been a leading source of health information for more than one hundred years. Supported by more than 35 million volunteers globally, we fund groundbreaking research, advocate for the public’s health, and provide critical resources to save and improve lives affected by cardiovascular disease and stroke. By driving breakthroughs and implementing proven solutions in science, policy, and care, we work tirelessly to advance health and transform lives every day. Connect with us on heart.org, Facebook, X or by calling 1-800-AHA-USA1.

content_html_zh:
声明要点：

  • 最新研究表明，每天摄入最多 400 毫克咖啡因（相当于每天最多 5、8 盎司杯含咖啡因的咖啡）对于大多数成年人来说通常是安全的，并且似乎可以降低某些人患多种心血管疾病的风险，包括心力衰竭、心脏病和中风。
  • 然而，较高含量的咖啡因（例如能量饮料（包括能量饮料）中的咖啡因含量）可能会增加患高血压和/或心律不齐或心律失常的风险。
  • 根据科学声明，需要更多的研究来更好地了解咖啡因对身体的影响、不同来源的咖啡因如何影响心脏健康以及其影响在不同人之间有何不同。

禁运至凌晨 4:00 CT / 上午 5:00 美国东部时间 2026 年 7 月 20 日星期一

达拉斯，2026 年 7 月 20 日——此前的研究发现，咖啡因是世界上最受欢迎的兴奋剂和精神活性物质，而咖啡是人们摄入咖啡因的最常见方式。 最新研究支持这样的结论：对于大多数成年人来说，每天摄入最多 400 毫克咖啡因（或每天最多 5、8 盎司杯含咖啡因的咖啡）是安全的，并且似乎可以降低某些人患心血管疾病的风险。 然而，根据美国心脏协会今天发表在美国心脏协会旗舰同行评审科学期刊《循环》上的一项新科学声明《咖啡因与心血管疾病》，较高剂量的咖啡因（例如能量饮料中的咖啡因含量，包括能量注射液）可能会导致心血管损害。

科学声明志愿者写作小组主席 Gregory M. 马库斯，医学博士、硕士、FAHA，加州大学旧金山分校医学院医学教授，加州大学旧金山分校健康中心心脏病学研究副主任。 “然而，高剂量的咖啡因，例如能量饮料中的咖啡因，可能会对心脏产生有害影响，应该避免。”

该声明的作者指出，研究咖啡因对心血管系统的影响具有挑战性。 在大多数研究中，咖啡是咖啡因的主要来源，观察到的心血管影响可能是由于咖啡中的其他化合物造成的。 例如，实验研究表明咖啡中的生物活性化合物可能具有抗氧化和抗炎特性，这可能有助于解释一些健康益处。 也很难将咖啡因的影响与通常添加到咖啡中的其他成分（例如牛奶、奶油、调味糖浆和/或糖）分开。 此外，大多数关于咖啡因的研究都是观察性的，这意味着它们无法证明因果关系。

虽然咖啡是大多数成年人咖啡因的主要来源，但茶、巧克力、苏打水和能量饮料以及非处方药和处方药也有助于咖啡因的总体摄入量。 需要对其他含有咖啡因的产品进行更多研究，以了解咖啡因含量和咖啡因对心血管健康的影响。

新的科学声明详细介绍了主要针对含咖啡因咖啡的最新研究，以及咖啡中咖啡因对高血压、胆固醇、2型糖尿病、冠心病、中风、心力衰竭、心房颤动、其他心律失常和其他心血管疾病的潜在影响。

咖啡因和心血管健康：需要了解的关键事项

  • 根据最新审查的研究结果，对于大多数成年人来说，每天摄入最多 400 毫克咖啡因，或每天不超过 3 到 5 杯 8 盎司的普通黑咖啡因咖啡（不含糖、甜味剂、香料或添加填充剂）被认为是安全的。 常规冲泡的非特制含咖啡因咖啡通常每盎司含有 9.4-20.6 毫克咖啡因。
  • 饮用不添加糖、香料或奶油的含咖啡因咖啡可以降低患 2 型糖尿病、心脏病、中风、心力衰竭和某些心律不齐的风险。
  • 随机试验是最严格、最可靠的研究设计类型，表明咖啡中的咖啡因摄入与心房颤动的较低风险相关，但也与室性早搏的较高风险相关。
  • 在咖啡中添加糖、调味糖浆、牛奶和/或奶油可能会降低其潜在的健康益处，需要更多的研究来了解这些添加剂的影响。
  • 较高的咖啡因摄入量（例如能量饮料中的咖啡因含量）与心血管危害相关，例如高血压和心律不齐的风险增加。 每盎司能量饮料可能含有 40-69 毫克咖啡因，是普通含咖啡因咖啡的 3-4 倍。

咖啡因如何影响身体？

咖啡中的咖啡因由肝脏代谢，对于大多数人来说通常在一小时内达到峰值浓度。 然而，个人处理咖啡因的速度取决于他们的遗传、新陈代谢、年龄和过去的咖啡因使用情况。 当经常饮用更多含咖啡因的咖啡时，有些人可能会对更高含量的咖啡因产生耐受性。 其他人可能会从相同量的咖啡因中感受到更强的效果。 咖啡因的短期影响可能包括暂时升高血压、心率、血糖和警觉性。 有些人可能会出现心悸和/或睡眠中断。

咖啡因对健康的具体影响：

  • 对血压的影响：研究发现，饮用含咖啡因的咖啡可能会对血压产生不同的影响，具体取决于饮用量。 对于血压处于最佳水平的人来说，每天喝 1-3 杯会增加患高血压的风险，而每天喝 3 杯以上则会降低患高血压的风险。 高剂量的咖啡因（例如能量饮料或能量注射中的咖啡因）会显着升高血压，尤其是对于已经患有高血压的人。
  • 对 2 型糖尿病的影响：研究表明，含咖啡因的咖啡可能会在短期内降低胰岛素敏感性。 此外，经常饮用含咖啡因的黑咖啡（不含添加剂、香料或甜味剂）与降低患 2 型糖尿病的风险有关。 然而，这种好处可能是由于咖啡中除咖啡因以外的化合物。 需要更多的研究来阐明含咖啡因的咖啡与咖啡中的各种化合物对 2 型糖尿病的影响。
  • 对胆固醇的影响：随机临床试验的数据表明，咖啡醇（含咖啡因和不含咖啡因的咖啡中的一种成分）与较高水平的低密度脂蛋白（也称为“坏”胆固醇）有关。 咖啡醇存在于未过滤的咖啡（例如浓缩咖啡、法式滤压咖啡、土耳其咖啡或煮咖啡）中，但不存在于用滤纸冲泡的咖啡或速溶咖啡中。 需要更多的研究来了解哪些产品的咖啡醇含量较高及其机制。
  • 对心律的影响：最新的健康测量分析以及参与者自我报告的信息发现，每天喝 1-3 杯含咖啡因的咖啡与心房颤动或心律异常的风险增加无关。 然而，相同水平的含咖啡因咖啡可能与心脏下腔更多的早期搏动有关，称为室性早搏（或 PVC）。 极高剂量的咖啡因（例如含咖啡因的能量饮料或能量注射液中的咖啡因）与通常被认为心血管疾病风险较低的人群（例如 30 岁以下的健康成年人）心律失常有关。
  • 对心脏病和中风的影响：每天喝 2-4 杯含咖啡因的咖啡可以降低患心脏病、心力衰竭和中风的风险。 然而，每天喝超过 4 杯含咖啡因的咖啡可能会增加心力衰竭的风险。

并非所有咖啡因的来源都是相同的

需要更多的研究来调查各种类型的含咖啡因咖啡和其他含有咖啡因的产品，如茶、苏打水、能量饮料和能量饮料以及食物对健康的不同影响。 针对茶与咖啡的研究较少。然而，与含咖啡因的咖啡类似，茶可以降低心房颤动、心力衰竭和中风的风险。 能量饮料、能量棒、凝胶和咖啡因补充剂通常含有其他添加成分，这些成分可能会增加咖啡因的吸收，可能会加速其对身体的影响，可能会升高血压并增加心血管伤害的风险。

需要更多随机对照试验来调查咖啡和其他含有咖啡因的产品中的咖啡因对心血管健康的影响，以更好地了解咖啡因如何影响不同的人以及不同来源的咖啡因如何影响心脏健康。 写作小组强调，还需要针对含咖啡因的能量饮料和其他含有咖啡因的流行产品进行研究。 由于缺乏足够、严格的数据来制定咖啡因摄入建议指南，该协会的 2026 年改善心血管健康饮食指南中没有提及咖啡因。

“尽管研究表明，对某些人来说，摄入咖啡因可能与某些心血管益处有关，但重要的是要记住，安全摄入咖啡因并没有‘一刀切’的策略。 人们对咖啡因的反应可能因多种因素而异，例如年龄、药物、潜在健康状况、遗传以及身体代谢咖啡因的速度。 对一个人来说合理的剂量可能会对另一个人造成不良影响，例如心悸、焦虑或睡眠中断。 这就是为什么重要的是要注意你的身体对咖啡因的反应，并与你的医疗团队讨论什么适合你，”马库斯说。

这份科学声明是由代表美国心脏协会生活方式和心脏代谢健康委员会的志愿者写作小组编写的；临床心脏病学委员会；和中风委员会。 美国心脏协会的科学声明提高了人们对心血管疾病和中风问题的认识，并有助于做出明智的医疗保健决策。 科学陈述概述了当前对某个主题的了解以及哪些领域需要进一步研究。 虽然科学陈述为指南的制定提供了信息，但它们并没有提出治疗建议。 美国心脏协会指南提供了该协会的官方临床实践建议。

共同作者是副主席 Frank B. 胡，医学博士，公共卫生硕士，博士，FAHA；罗布·M. 范达姆博士；玛丽莲·C. 科内利斯博士；托马斯·A. 杜兰，医学博士； JungHee Kang，博士、公共卫生硕士、注册护士；苏珊娜·C. 拉尔森博士；罗伯特·L。 第二页，药学博士、硕士、FAHA； Niyati Parekh 博士，FAHA。 作者的披露内容已在手稿中列出。

该协会 85% 以上的收入来自公司以外的来源。 这些来源包括个人、基金会和遗产的捐款，以及投资收益和销售我们的教育材料的收入。 企业（包括制药、设备制造商和其他公司）也向协会捐款。 该协会制定了严格的政策，防止任何捐赠影响其科学内容和政策立场。 总体财务信息可在此处获取。

关于美国心脏协会

美国心脏协会是致力于实现更长寿、更健康的世界的不懈力量。 该组织致力于确保所有社区的公平健康，一百多年来一直是健康信息的主要来源。 在全球超过 3500 万志愿者的支持下，我们资助突破性研究，倡导公众健康，并提供关键资源来拯救和改善受心血管疾病和中风影响的生命。 通过在科学、政策和护理方面推动突破并实施行之有效的解决方案，我们每天不懈地努力促进健康并改变生活。 通过 heart.org、Facebook、X 或致电 1-800-AHA-USA1 与我们联系。
